_description: Single source of truth for the approved GLICE research task. Chat context and attachments are not authoritative.

research_contract.hypothesis: Local event identity (DICEC/TICEC) is insufficient for BBS trio hard rows because event ambiguity requires cross-segment consistency. A lightweight cluster-level latent identity state — not full alignment, not graph, not beam — can resolve cross-segment ambiguity while preserving observed-only constraints.
research_contract.mechanism_summary: Maintain a ClusterIdentityState containing event identity candidates, segment compatibility matrix, event persistence scores, contradiction scores, and a global consistency score. Update incrementally per read. Decode constrained by identity state rather than independent per-segment decisions.
research_contract.quality_gate: ED <= DICEC AND accuracy >= DICEC AND exact >= DICEC

target_gates.quality: ED <= DICEC (4.2793) AND accuracy >= DICEC (0.9661) AND exact >= DICEC (0.3486)

protected_boundary.old_results: must not be overwritten; only append

parent_context.ticEC_finding: TICEC output similarity 0.9935 vs DICEC; temporal identity provides negligible improvement; functionally duplicate
parent_context.primary_bottleneck: BBS trio hard rows have event identity ambiguity that local anchor-segment decisions cannot resolve

glice_summary.conclusion: Cluster-level identity state with cross-segment compatibility provides marginal improvements over DICEC but does not break the information boundary. The anchor-segment-local paradigm cannot resolve BBS trio hard rows. Further progress requires fundamentally different observation models or global path information that exceeds observed-only constraints.
